differentials.name: Perineal hernia (rare) (a)  anterior perineal hernia = defect of urogenital diaphragm anterior to superficial transverse perineal m. + lateral to bulbocavernosus m. + medial to ischiocavernosus m. (only in females) (b)  posterior perineal hernia = defect in levator ani m. / between levator ani m.

(only in females) (b)  posterior perineal hernia = defect in levator ani m. / between levator ani m.

differentials.name: Supravesical  6% An internal hernia is diagnosed by its saclike closed-loop appearance and displacement of vessels and surrounding structures around the hernia orifice and sac.
